heading: Maritime intelligence
label: 70113 Maritime intelligence
notes: Amendments2004—Subsec. (a). inserted at end “The system may include a vessel risk profiling component that assigns incoming vessels a terrorism risk rating.”Pub. L. 108–293
amendments: Amendments2004—Subsec. (a). inserted at end “The system may include a vessel risk profiling component that assigns incoming vessels a terrorism risk rating.”Pub. L. 108–293

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: The Secretary shall implement a system to collect, integrate, and analyze information concerning vessels operating on or bound for waters subject to the jurisdiction of the United States, including information related to crew, passengers, cargo, and intermodal shipments. The system may include a vessel risk profiling component that assigns incoming vessels a terrorism risk rating.

children.heading: .—Consultation
children.label: b .—Consultation
children.text: In developing the information system under subsection (a), the Secretary shall consult with the Transportation Security Oversight Board and other departments and agencies, as appropriate.

children.heading: .—Information Integration
children.label: c .—Information Integration
children.text: To deter a transportation security incident, the Secretary may collect information from public and private entities to the extent that the information is not provided by other Federal departments and agencies.
